chunk_summary: This section outlines tele-nutrition strategies for pediatric obesity in India, focusing on obesity classification, comorbidity screening, and follow-up intensity using WHO and IAP growth charts, risk-based treatment, and escalation protocols.
learning_objectives: Understand the use of WHO and IAP growth charts for pediatric obesity classification in India.
Identify comorbidities and escalation thresholds for in-person assessments in tele-nutrition workflows.
Design risk-based tele-nutrition follow-up schedules for pediatric obesity management.
Implement stage-matched, family-based lifestyle interventions for behavior change.
key_facts: WHO growth charts are used for children under 5 years, and IAP 2015 charts for ages 5–18 years.
Small height errors can distort BMI-for-age (BAZ) calculations, emphasizing the need for accurate height measurement.
Escalation thresholds include rapid weight gain, sleep-disordered breathing, suspected hypertension, and insulin resistance symptoms.
Baseline assessments should include vitals, pubertal staging, and labs (lipids, glucose, liver enzymes) based on severity and age.
Tele-nutrition schedules should include weekly or fortnightly sessions for 4–6 weeks, followed by monthly maintenance.
Stage-matched goals include one change each in beverage, snack, activity, and sleep routines.
reference_quote: Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Treatment of Children and Adolescents With Obesity (AAP): 'Multi-component, family-based lifestyle interventions are the cornerstone of pediatric obesity care.'
document_key_topics: Obesity classification using WHO and IAP charts
Family-based lifestyle interventions for pediatric obesity
